ogTitle: Granville Harbour vs Grassy: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Granville Harbour and Grassy.
heading: Granville Harbour vs Grassy — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Granville Harbour, Tasmania and Grassy, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Grassy has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Granville Harbour (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Granville Harbour or Grassy safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Granville Harbour has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Grassy scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Granville Harbour scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Grassy scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Granville Harbour or Grassy?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Grassy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
